highlights: Established the JeffersonWM homepage as the live hub for the site network.
Integrated the right-side widget flow with cleaner weather, clock, and word-of-the-day behavior.
Set the groundwork for app links, deployment notes, and the new feed entry point.
Restored the preferred live version of the frontend experience.
Moved image delivery back onto the home-server model through the dedicated API.
Cleaned up reveal behavior, board sizing, and footer visibility.
Converted the placeholder starter into the real archive site.
Split the stack into a static ASO frontend and a home-server image API.
Brought the archive image flow online through api.jeffershizzle.com.
Normalized the service around port 8040 and api-lionship.jeffersonwm.com.
Stabilized the database-backed links flow and status endpoints.
Became the canonical backend for JeffersonWM widget events and daily word data.
Completed the v0.1 foundation with the Vite plus Express runtime and SQLite data model.
Built the core RPG loop with XP, HP, gold, stats, streaks, and achievement tracking.
Shipped the admin board, public dashboard, and three playable mini-games as the first working gameplay surface.
Added Vermilion as a desktop file organizer for sorting and batch-renaming large image directories.
Documented preset-driven distribution workflows and preview tooling.
Included Windows Explorer integration and no-dependency Python setup.
Expanded the archive from an image-only browser into a mixed file and media explorer.
Added optional folder thumbnails, richer file handling, and metadata foundations.
Connected the private archive flow to the shared Auth JeffersonWM account system.
Launched the standalone central auth service for the JeffersonWM network.
Added request approval, app memberships, admin notes, and filtered account history.
Enabled shared jeffersonwm.com sessions so protected apps can trust one login.
Launched the standalone JeffersonWM feed page and api-feed.jeffersonwm.com service.
Mixed manual release notes into the live GitHub timeline chronologically.
Added a dedicated changelog view so semantic version updates can be read as their own lane.
